ocr.text: The following table presents an analysis of various structural data related to the assessment of changes in Best Corrected Visual
Acuity (BCVA) across different experimental methods. The data highlights the relationships between specific metrics such as
the resolution of structures and the sequence identity percentage (Seq Id %) for several experimental conditions. Notably, the
integration of both conventional techniques and advanced methods, evidenced by the columns labeled "SAAT" and
"Conventional," allows for a comprehensive evaluation of the structural data obtained from diverse organisms.
Retinectomy extension  Total
Change in BCVA
Worsening  0  3  2  5
Better or no change  10  4  19  33
Total  10  7  21  38
PDB file  Ligand  Method  Resolution  Seq Id %  NNT domain  Organism
2bru  NAD, NADP  NMR  –  56  I, I‐I, I‐III  E. coli
4izh  Glycerol  X‐ray  1.8 Å  41  I  T. thermophilus
4o9p (chains C‐D)  –  X‐ray  2.89 Å  31  II  T. thermophilus
1djl  NADP, sulfate ion, glycerol  X‐ray  2.0 Å  100  III  Homo sapiens
4o9u  NAD, NADP  X‐ray  6.93 Å  33  Holo‐NNT  T. thermophilus
No. of explants  Kanr  GFP
SAAT  Conventional  SAAT  Conventional
The ligands and PDB file identifiers included in the table facilitate a direct correlation between the structural parameters and
their potential implications for BCVA outcomes. By synthesizing data from methods such as NMR and comparing resolution
metrics, researchers can draw more informed conclusions regarding the efficacy of various ligands in influencing visual acuity.
This comparative analysis underscores the importance of methodological choice in structural biology and its direct
ramifications on clinical outcomes related to visual health.
